texts.full_text: heat shock protein (HSP), which bind and inactivate excess steroid hormones inside receptor target tissues or other organs before they reach damaging levels to protect cells from harm caused due their high reactivity when unbound with receptors; this mechanism ensures only appropriate amounts are released into circulation at any given time. The SSH then diffuse through the cell membrane of a responsive tissue, where it binds specific intracellular proteinaceous (steroid) hormone receptor complexes within seconds afterexposure - these binding events trigger gene expression changes resulti
